bead.title: modelregistry: carry endpoint and harness identity in KnownModel
bead.description:
CONTEXT
SD-005 requires auto-routing to consume the same enriched available-model snapshot exposed by `fiz models`. The current `internal/modelregistry.KnownModel` is centered on `(provider, model)` and lacks enough dispatch identity for multi-endpoint routing: harness, provider type, endpoint name/base URL, server instance, billing/default-inclusion evidence, and route-facing capability fields. This blocks replacing the duplicate service routing discovery path.

CHANGE
Extend `internal/modelregistry.KnownModel` and snapshot assembly so each discovered row carries the identity/facts needed to expand into a dispatch candidate. Preserve the operator-facing canonical provider/model identity while representing multiple endpoint/server-instance rows when a provider has multiple endpoints serving the same model. Subscription harness rows must retain harness-as-provider identity.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- internal/modelregistry/types.go
- internal/modelregistry/assemble.go
- internal/modelregistry/discovery.go
- internal/modelregistry/*_test.go
- agentcli/models.go and tests only as needed to render/assert new JSON fields

OUT-OF-SCOPE
- Rewiring ResolveRoute or RouteStatus to consume the snapshot.
- Changing scoring behavior.
- Removing legacy service discovery helpers; that is a downstream cleanup bead.
bead.acceptance:
1. `go test ./internal/modelregistry/... -run "KnownModel|Assemble|Endpoint|Harness" -count=1` passes and includes a multi-endpoint fixture proving distinct rows preserve endpoint_name, endpoint_base_url, and server_instance for the same provider/model.
2. `go test ./agentcli -run "TestModelsListJSONEmitsSnapshot|TestModelsDetailRuntimeFields" -count=1` passes and asserts the new identity fields remain present alongside quota_remaining/recent latency fields.
3. `rg -n "EndpointName|EndpointBaseURL|ServerInstance|Harness" internal/modelregistry/types.go agentcli/models.go` shows the enriched model identity fields are part of the unified snapshot surface.
